items.contextualized_question: Company name
items.question: Company name
items.extracted_answer: Peak Reinsurance Company Limited is a Hong Kong-based global reinsurance company

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] What is the legal/registered name of the company?
items.question: Legal name
items.extracted_answer: Peak Reinsurance Company Limited ("Peak Re" or the "Company").

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] Where is the company headquartered, and what other offices does it operate?
items.question: Headquarters
items.extracted_answer: Headquartered in Hong Kong at 15/F, WKCDA Tower, No. 8 Austin Road West, West Kowloon Cultural District, Kowloon, Hong Kong. Additional office: Peak Reinsurance AG, Fortunagasse 28, 3rd Floor, 8001 Zurich, Switzerland.

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] In what year did the company commence operations?
items.question: Year established

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] What industry does the company operate in, and what products and services does it offer?
items.question: Business / industry
items.extracted_answer: Global reinsurance. Peak Re offers both Property & Casualty (P&C) and Life & Health (L&H) reinsurance, and provides innovative and tailored reinsurance, risk management and capital management solutions to clients around the world.

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] Which regulator authorises the company and under which ordinance?
items.question: Regulator
items.extracted_answer: Authorised by the Insurance Authority of Hong Kong under the Insurance Ordinance (Cap. 41).

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] Who are the major shareholders and what are their ownership percentages?
items.question: Ownership
items.extracted_answer: Fosun International Limited (00656.HK) owns approximately 87% and Prudential Financial, Inc. owns approximately 13%, both held through Peak Reinsurance Holdings Limited.

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] What is the company's current financial strength rating?
items.question: Credit rating
items.extracted_answer: A- by AM Best.

items.contextualized_question: [Company Profile] How does the company rank within its global industry?
items.question: Industry ranking
items.extracted_answer: 27th among global reinsurance groups in terms of net reinsurance premiums written (S&P Global Ratings Top 40 Global Reinsurers, 2023).

items.contextualized_question: [Financial Performance (FY2023)] What was reinsurance revenue for the year ended 31 December 2023?
items.question: Reinsurance revenue
items.extracted_answer: USD 1.56 billion.

items.contextualized_question: [Financial Performance (FY2023)] What was net profit for the year ended 31 December 2023?
items.question: Net profit
items.extracted_answer: USD 200 million.

items.contextualized_question: [Financial Performance (FY2023)] What were net assets as at 31 December 2023?
items.question: Net assets
items.extracted_answer: USD 1.28 billion.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] What was the total number of employees at year-end 2023?
items.question: Headcount
items.extracted_answer: Over 130 employees.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] How many nationalities are represented in the workforce?
items.question: Nationalities
items.extracted_answer: 18 nationalities.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] How many languages are spoken across the workforce?
items.question: Languages
items.extracted_answer: 21 languages.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] What proportion of functional leadership roles are held by women?
items.question: Women in leadership
items.extracted_answer: 45% of leadership roles are held by women.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] What proportion of new hires in 2023 were women?
items.question: Women among new hires
items.extracted_answer: 42% of new hires are women.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] What proportion of employees promoted in 2023 were women?
items.question: Women among promotions
items.extracted_answer: 55% of promoted employees in 2023 were women.

items.contextualized_question: [Workforce & Diversity] Does the company have an anti-discrimination commitment, and how is it implemented?
items.question: Anti-discrimination
items.extracted_answer: The company's code of conduct prohibits discrimination on any grounds, and the company follows anti-discrimination ordinances in the different jurisdictions where it operates. A mandatory compliance training module on "Diversity, Equality, and Inclusion" was introduced in 2023.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] How is the Board structured, and what proportion of independent non-executive directors does it include?
items.question: Board structure
items.extracted_answer: The Board upholds the highest standards of corporate governance in accordance with the requirements of the Hong Kong Insurance Authority (HKIA). Composition includes a higher percentage of independent non-executive directors (INEDs) than required by the HKIA, and all Board committees are chaired by INEDs. Director appointments are subject to regulatory approvals.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] What committees support the Board?
items.question: Board committees
items.extracted_answer: Four committees: (1) Audit committee; (2) Investment committee; (3) Risk management and compliance committee; (4) Nomination and remuneration committee. All four are chaired by INEDs.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] How often does the Board meet and what was director attendance in 2023?
items.question: Board meetings and attendance
items.extracted_answer: The Board meets at least four times per year, or more frequently as required. Average director attendance at quarterly board meetings was 96% in 2023.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] Who is responsible for ESG governance within the company?
items.question: ESG governance ownership
items.extracted_answer: Responsibility for ESG governance sits with the executive committee, as delegated by the Board. Accountability for ESG integration is taken up by multiple functions — risk management (ESG risk assessment and regulatory requirements), analytics (climate risk assessment), the investment team (ESG integration in investment portfolios), corporate communications (ESG disclosures), and research (emerging ESG risks and opportunities). Some functions have appointed dedicated ESG personnel.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] Describe the company's risk management model.
items.question: Three Lines of Defence
items.extracted_answer: The "Three Lines of Defence" model underpins the governance and risk management framework. First line: operating units that design processes and internal control systems. Second line: compliance, legal and risk management functions providing independent oversight of risk-taking and risk-management activities. Third line: internal audit function providing independent validation of controls in the first two lines.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] Who is the current Chief Executive Officer?
items.question: CEO
items.extracted_answer: Franz-Joseph Hahn.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] What mandatory compliance trainings are provided to employees?
items.question: Compliance training
items.extracted_answer: Group-wide mandatory trainings on anti-money laundering (AML), counter-terrorist financing (CTF), anti-bribery and anti-corruption, data protection, sanctions compliance, and (from 2023) Diversity, Equality and Inclusion.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] Has the Board received ESG training?
items.question: Board ESG training
items.extracted_answer: Yes — in 2023, Board members received ESG-focused training to deepen their knowledge on ESG topics and stay abreast of the latest developments.

items.contextualized_question: [Governance] Who holds final authority on exceptions to underwriting guidelines?
items.question: Chief Underwriting Officer authority
items.extracted_answer: The Chief Underwriting Officer holds the final decision on guideline exceptions.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] What are the pillars of the company's sustainability framework?
items.question: Sustainability framework pillars
items.extracted_answer: Environmental (Physical Risk, Transition Risk, Liability Risks), Social (Working Conditions, Human Rights), Governance (Compliance, Anti-bribery / Anti-Corruption, Governance).

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] Is the company a signatory to the UNEP FI Principles for Sustainable Insurance, and since when?
items.question: UNEP FI PSI membership
items.extracted_answer: Yes — Peak Re has been a signatory to the UNEP FI Principles for Sustainable Insurance (PSI) since 2012, and was among the early signatories at its launch.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] Is the company a signatory to the Hong Kong Insurance Industry Climate Charter?
items.question: Hong Kong Climate Charter
items.extracted_answer: Yes — in February 2024, Peak Re became one of 33 inaugural signatories to the Hong Kong Insurance Industry Climate Charter ("Climate Charter") developed by the Hong Kong Federation of Insurers (HKFI). Under this commitment, Peak Re will align with the Hong Kong Climate Action Plan 2050 and Paris Agreement goals, and publish Scope 1 and 2 interim targets by 2026.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] What industry bodies and initiatives is the company part of?
items.question: Industry engagements
items.extracted_answer: HKFI Task-force on Green Insurance; Hong Kong's Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group (CASG); Fosun ESG Ambassadors program (from 2024); ongoing collaboration since 2015 with the Shanghai Typhoon Institute (STI) and Shanghai Central Meteorological Observatory (SCMO) on cyclone monitoring.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] Has the company conducted a materiality assessment, and what approach was used?
items.question: Materiality assessment
items.extracted_answer: Yes — a double-materiality assessment was conducted in 2023-24 to inform the ESG initiatives and workplan for the coming years. Approach: (1) Long list of ESG factors (industry-specific considerations, current and emerging ESG topics, peer practices); (2) Stakeholder engagement via interviews and online employee surveys; (3) Final assessment with risk and finance functions ranking impact and likelihood; (4) Consolidate and validate shortlisted topics with leadership.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] What are the top 5 material ESG topics identified?
items.question: Top 5 material ESG topics
items.extracted_answer: (1) Increase in climate-related physical risk; (2) Decarbonisation; (3) Ageing population; (4) ESG risks and opportunities oversight; (5) Economic development and "Just transition" in emerging markets.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] Does the company publish an annual ESG/sustainability report?
items.question: ESG disclosure reporting
items.extracted_answer: Yes — Peak Re publishes an annual ESG Disclosure Report as part of its PSI membership commitment to transparency and accountability. This document is the 2023 edition.

items.contextualized_question: [ESG Strategy & Frameworks] Is the company aligning with IFRS S1/S2 sustainability disclosure standards?
items.question: IFRS sustainability standards alignment
items.extracted_answer: Yes — Peak Re has conducted a gap analysis against IFRS S1 (General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-related Financial Information) and IFRS S2 (Climate-related Disclosures), introduced by the ISSB in 2023 and adopted into Hong Kong's ESG disclosure requirements. The company is discussing ways to narrow these gaps in preparation for future adoption.

items.contextualized_question: [Underwriting & Investment Screening] What screening framework does the company apply to its underwriting and investment activities?
items.question: IFC Performance Standards
items.extracted_answer: The eight IFC Performance Standards (PS1–PS8): PS1 Assessment and management of environmental and social risks and impacts; PS2 Labour and working conditions; PS3 Resource efficiency and pollution prevention; PS4 Community health, safety and security; PS5 Land acquisition and involuntary resettlement; PS6 Biodiversity conservation and sustainable management of living natural resources; PS7 Indigenous peoples; PS8 Cultural heritage.

items.contextualized_question: [Underwriting & Investment Screening] What sectors / activities are on the company's exclusion list?
items.question: Exclusion list
items.extracted_answer: Illegal production or trading (PS1); weapons and munitions (PS4); alcoholic beverages (PS4); tobacco (PS4); gambling and casinos (PS4); radioactive materials (PS3/PS4/PS6); asbestos fibers (PS3/PS4); drift net fishing (PS6); human rights, forced or child labour (PS2); forestry and logging (PS5/PS6/PS7/PS8).

items.contextualized_question: [Underwriting & Investment Screening] What additional climate-related exclusions were enacted?
items.question: Climate-related exclusions (2023)
items.extracted_answer: From 2023, facultative reinsurance excluded for new construction of coal-based power plants or coal mines, and for projects directly involved in exploration, drilling or production of oil and gas from the Arctic Basin, or from oil or tar sands. Exceptions only where the counterparty is Paris-Agreement aligned with credible transition plans, or where social needs for power generation are significant (e.g. developing countries with low electrification rates).

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What are the company's Scope 1 (direct) operational emissions?
items.question: Scope 1 emissions
items.extracted_answer: Negligible for 2023. Peak Re does not own any building/stationary combustion sources or company cars.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What are the company's Scope 2 (indirect, purchased electricity) emissions?
items.question: Scope 2 emissions
items.extracted_answer: Estimated at 0.81 tonnes CO2e/FTE for the Hong Kong office in 2023, with average electricity consumption of 2,074 kWh/FTE.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What are the emissions from business travel?
items.question: Business travel emissions
items.extracted_answer: For 2023, employee flight mileage was estimated at 8,375 miles/employee, with an emissions intensity estimate of 1.35 tonnes CO2e/employee.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What emissions targets has the company committed to?
items.question: Emissions targets
items.extracted_answer: Under the HKFI Climate Charter (Feb 2024), Peak Re committed to publishing Scope 1 and 2 interim targets by 2026, aligning with the Hong Kong Climate Action Plan 2050 and Paris Agreement goals. An emissions baselining exercise for the Hong Kong headquarters was initiated in 2024.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What initiatives has the company undertaken to improve energy efficiency?
items.question: Energy efficiency initiatives
items.extracted_answer: Relocated the Hong Kong headquarters in November 2022 to WKCDA Tower — a BEAM Plus Gold certified energy-efficient complex with reduced lighting consumption, motion and presence sensors for lighting controls, and temperature sensors that adjust to occupancy and natural light levels. Adjusted for square footage, the new office provides about 15% greater energy efficiency.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] What green-building certifications does the company hold?
items.question: Green-building certifications
items.extracted_answer: BEAM Plus Gold (building); LEED Gold (Interior Design and Construction — Commercial Interiors, January 2023); WELL Certification – Gold (February 2024).

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] How does the company assess physical and transition climate risks?
items.question: Climate risk assessment
items.extracted_answer: Physical risk: strict underwriting guidelines and stringent geographic and probable maximum loss (PML) requirements; diversification and retrocession cover; advanced risk modeling and data analytics including internal research on secondary perils (flash floods, hail, droughts, wildfires, landslides); collaboration with the Shanghai Typhoon Institute and Shanghai Central Meteorological Observatory. Transition risk: considered limited; accommodated through annual contract renewals, regular reviews of underwriting assumptions, new business opportunities, and exclusion of coal, Arctic oil/gas, and tar sands. Both risks are stress-tested annually through the Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) exercise.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] How is the investment portfolio screened for transition risks?
items.question: Investment portfolio ESG screening
items.extracted_answer: A preliminary assessment using the MSCI ESG database shows limited exposure to high carbon emission sectors (transport, utilities, construction) and companies lagging in ESG action, with the caveat that data availability on the regional fixed-income portfolio was limited and subject to change. Peak Re intends to further work with asset managers to enhance the portfolio's sustainability profile.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] Has the company issued any catastrophe bonds?
items.question: Catastrophe bond
items.extracted_answer: Yes — in 2022, Peak Re was the first reinsurer based in Hong Kong to issue a 144A catastrophe bond. The USD 150 million issuance attracted significant investor interest, allowed a 100% upsize from the initially announced size, and enabled a multi-year retrocession agreement covering typhoon risk in Japan.

items.contextualized_question: [Climate & Environment] Has the company raised any notable reinsurance capacity vehicles?
items.question: Asia's first sidecar
items.extracted_answer: Peak Re raised Asia's first sidecar, bringing greater reinsurance capacity to the region and helping to close protection gaps in emerging markets.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] What agricultural insurance programmes does the company support?
items.question: Agricultural insurance
items.extracted_answer: Large-scale agriculture schemes in India and Thailand (including the Thai rice scheme), covering crop failure and reducing premium burden on farmers to safeguard incomes from extreme weather, price volatility, production risks (diseases, pests), and natural disasters. In China, supports farmers' houses and agriculture programmes.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] What microinsurance programmes does the company participate in?
items.question: Microinsurance
items.extracted_answer: The CARD Pioneer microinsurance scheme in the Philippines, providing affordable natural catastrophe protection to socially and economically challenged families with limited access to insurance.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] What health-related inclusive insurance initiatives does the company support?
items.question: Health insurance initiatives
items.extracted_answer: Hospice care insurance in China (end-of-life care, family/carer support); hypertensive severe complications insurance for people with pre-existing hypertensive conditions (covering strokes, severe retinal disease, renal failure).

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] Does the company support any worker-protection insurance products?
items.question: Worker protection
items.extracted_answer: Yes — supports worker's salary bond in China, protecting construction workers from non-payment or delayed salary payments by contracting companies.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] Does the company participate in regional disaster-risk facilities?
items.question: Regional disaster facilities
items.extracted_answer: Yes — participates in the Southeast Asia Disaster Risk Insurance Facility (SEADRIF), a partnership of ASEAN+3 (10 ASEAN nations plus Japan, China, South Korea) and the World Bank providing pre-disaster planning and post-disaster relief funding to enhance financial resilience.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] Does the company participate in natural peril pools?
items.question: Natural peril pools
items.extracted_answer: Yes — participates in several natural peril pools globally, including in Europe and the United Kingdom (supporting flood insurance affordability and availability), providing affordable protection in high-risk areas.

items.contextualized_question: [Products & Social Impact] Which UN Sustainable Development Goals does the company align with?
items.question: SDG alignment
items.extracted_answer: SDG 1 (No Poverty), SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being), SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities), and SDG 13 (Climate Action). Partnerships for SDG 17 are also referenced.

items.contextualized_question: [Community & CSR] With which NGO has the company established a strategic CSR partnership?
items.question: CSR partner NGO
items.extracted_answer: CarbonCare Innolab (CCIL), a Hong Kong-based NGO, through a multi-event partnership in 2023-24 focused on climate solutions and community impact.

items.contextualized_question: [Community & CSR] What community education activities has the company participated in?
items.question: Community education activities
items.extracted_answer: Solar energy tour with SEN students from Mary Rose School, Hong Kong (17 May 2024, in partnership with CCIL); nutritional insights sessions; organic ecofarming day; low-carbon cooking with an accredited dietician; Biz-Green Dress Day; Tasse Coffee community partnership training disadvantaged communities with a focus on empowering women.

items.contextualized_question: [Community & CSR] Describe the Yongping community project.
items.question: Yongping tea project
items.extracted_answer: An ongoing poverty-alleviation and sustainable-development relationship with tea farmers in Yongping, China. Peak Re procures teas from the Dapingtan Tea Garden (organic, chemical-free farming) for corporate gifts. Staff package teas during annual events; consistent purchases assist villagers with household expenses and children's education.

items.contextualized_question: [Community & CSR] What volunteering activities has the company participated in?
items.question: Volunteering / heritage
items.extracted_answer: Blood donation drive with the Hong Kong Red Cross Blood Transfusion Service; Peak Capital Bermuda volunteered with the St. George Foundation to restore Fort St. Catherine (a UNESCO World Heritage Site and Bermuda's largest fortification).

items.contextualized_question: [Forward-Looking Roadmap] Over what horizon is the company developing its next ESG roadmap?
items.question: ESG strategy horizon
items.extracted_answer: A 3-5 year roadmap is being developed to advance ESG goals and commitments.

items.contextualized_question: [Forward-Looking Roadmap] What are the pillars of the "Advancing Our ESG Journey" roadmap?
items.question: Roadmap pillars
items.extracted_answer: Six pillars: IDENTIFY (resolve gaps between communicated and actual ESG practices); BUILD (capacity and training to meet ESG challenges); EXPAND (data collection to inform decision-making); UPDATE (risk analytics to identify, analyse and manage ESG risks); ELEVATE (embed ESG policies into respective functions); INTEGRATE (wider business strategies with ESG-related opportunities).

items.contextualized_question: [Forward-Looking Roadmap] How is the company approaching financed and underwriting emissions measurement?
items.question: Financed / underwriting emissions
items.extracted_answer: Gathering investment data via the MSCI ESG database and exploring collaborations with external fund managers for investment-related emissions. For underwriting emissions, in communication with the Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials (PCAF) as it develops guidelines and standards for treaty underwriting.
